1в†’# PRD: Standalone Apps Portfolio (v3 вЂ” pre-researched, no read-existing) 2в†’ 3в†’**Slug:** `standalone-apps-portfolio-v3` 4в†’**Project:** `website` (shipyard.company) 5в†’**Type:** Feature вЂ” content + routing 6в†’**Priority:** P1 7в†’**Date:** 2026-05-03 8в†’**Supersedes:** v1 (hollow under Kimi), v2 (hollow under qwen3.5 + glm-4.6 вЂ” read-existing PRD shape doesn't work) 9в†’ 10в†’--- 11в†’ 12в†’## Why v3 exists 13в†’ 14в†’v1 and v2 failed identically on three different models (kimi, qwen3.5, glm-4.6) because they instruct the build agent to "READ each `projects//` then synthesize descriptions." Across all models tested, that prompt shape produces zero `Write` tool calls. The builder reads, accumulates context, and never transitions to writing source files. 15в†’ 16в†’v3 removes the read-existing step. **All factual content about the 3 apps is already pre-researched and embedded in this PRD as literal copy-paste strings.** The build agent only needs to write what's already in this file. 17в†’ 18в†’--- 19в†’ 20в†’## Locked decisions (carried from v1 debate, see `rounds/standalone-apps-portfolio/decisions.md`) 21в†’ 22в†’- Static export, zero runtime 23в†’- No gradients, no pulses, no theater 24в†’- Single `portfolio.ts` consumed by every route 25в†’- `generateStaticParams` for `[slug]` 26в†’- Server Components only 27в†’- No screenshots / browser mockups in v1 28в†’ 29в†’## Required output (build gate enforces в‰Ґ3 .ts/.tsx files) 30в†’ 31в†’All work in `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/deliverables/standalone-apps-portfolio-v3/`: 32в†’ 33в†’1. `spec.md` вЂ” restate the 7 acceptance criteria below 34в†’2. `todo.md` вЂ” checklist (each file = one item) 35в†’3. `portfolio.ts` вЂ” exact contents specified below 36в†’4. `work-section.tsx` вЂ” drop-in JSX section for `/work` page 37в†’5. `portfolio-slug-page.tsx` вЂ” Next.js dynamic `[slug]` page 38в†’6. `tests/portfolio-data.test.ts` вЂ” node --test validation 39в†’7. `MIGRATION.md` вЂ” instructions for human integration 40в†’ 41в†’--- 42в†’ 43в†’## EXACT contents for `portfolio.ts` 44в†’ 45в†’Write this file verbatim. Do not modify the strings. These descriptions were verified against actual source code in `projects//` on 2026-05-03. 46в†’ 47в†’```ts 48в†’export type AppEntry = { 49в†’ slug: string; 50в†’ name: string; 51в†’ tagline: string; 52в†’ status: "SHIPPED" | "BUILD" | "SCAFFOLD"; 53в†’ github: string; 54в†’ features: string[]; 55в†’ techStack: string[]; 56в†’ accent: "amber" | "rose" | "teal" | "cyan" | "lime" | "fuchsia"; 57в†’}; 58в†’ 59в†’export const apps: AppEntry[] = [ 60в†’ { 61в†’ slug: "tuned", 62в†’ name: "Tuned", 63в†’ tagline: "Prompt version control for AI applications. Track, version, and roll out prompts across your stack.", 64в†’ status: "BUILD", 65в†’ github: "https://github.com/sethshoultes/shipyard-ai/tree/main/projects/tuned", 66в†’ features: [ 67в†’ "CLI (`tuned`) built on Commander for prompt push/pull/diff", 68в†’ "TypeScript SDK exported as `@tuned/sdk` with ESM-only build", 69в†’ "Cloudflare Worker storage backend (Wrangler-deployed)", 70в†’ "Dashboard for viewing prompt history and diffs", 71в†’ "Schema-first design (worker/schema.sql)" 72в†’ ], 73в†’ techStack: ["TypeScript", "Commander", "Cloudflare Workers", "Wrangler"], 74в†’ accent: "amber" 75в†’ }, 76в†’ { 77в†’ slug: "promptfolio", 78в†’ name: "Promptfolio", 79в†’ tagline: "Turn your Claude conversation exports into a shareable, indexed portfolio of prompts.", 80в†’ status: "BUILD", 81в†’ github: "https://github.com/sethshoultes/shipyard-ai/tree/main/projects/promptfolio", 82в†’ features: [ 83в†’ "Parses Claude `.json` exports via Zod-validated schema", 84в†’ "Renders markdown with `react-markdown` + GitHub-flavored remark", 85в†’ "Auto-generated OG images via `@vercel/og`", 86в†’ "Bulk export as `.zip` archive of selected prompts", 87в†’ "Next.js 14 App Router with server components" 88в†’ ], 89в†’ techStack: ["Next.js 14", "React 18", "Zod", "@vercel/og", "react-markdown"], 90в†’ accent: "rose" 91в†’ }, 92в†’ { 93в†’ slug: "commandbar", 94в†’ name: "Beam (Commandbar)", 95в†’ tagline: "WordPress command palette plugin. 1в†’# Board Review вЂ” Standalone Apps Portfolio v3
     2в†’**Reviewer:** Jensen Huang (NVIDIA / Great Minds Agency)
     3в†’**Verdict:** Ship rejected. Rebuild with a distribution layer or kill it.
     4в†’**Score:** 3/10 вЂ” Static brochure masquerading as strategy; fails own acceptance criteria.
     5в†’
     6в†’---
     7в†’
     8в†’## Moat
     9в†’- None. Zero.
    10в†’- Static Next.js pages. No data, no network effects, no lock-in.
    11в†’- Any junior dev replicates this in a day.
    12в†’
    13в†’## AI Leverage
    14в†’- Negative.
    15в†’- PRD pre-wrote exact strings to avoid hallucination.
    16в†’- Builder still improvised portfolio.ts вЂ” wrong taglines, wrong features, wrong tech stacks.
    17в†’- AI created rework, not 10x.
    18в†’
    19в†’## Unfair Advantage We're Not Building
    20в†’- No app marketplace.
    21в†’- No live deploy previews. No install button for Beam. No download metrics.
    22в†’- Not curating demand. Not building distribution.
    23в†’- Wasted asset: GitHub repos exist but page doesn't surface releases, stars, or real activity.
    24в†’
    25в†’## Platform vs Product
    26в†’- Product: "Here are 3 things we built."
    27в†’- Platform: "Install, fork, and extend every tool in one click."
    28в†’- Missing: One-click WordPress plugin install for Beam. Live demo sandbox for Promptfolio. CLI download badge for Tuned.
    29в†’- Missing: User-generated submissions. Community scaffolds. Networked catalog.
    30в†’
    31в†’---
    32в†’
    33в†’## Fixes or Kill
    34в†’- **Path A (Platform):** Turn /portfolio into app store. Add deploy buttons, live previews, usage analytics, community submissions. Then this compounds.
    35в†’- **Path B (Kill):** Merge into /work as 3 bullet points. Delete this PRD. Stop burning cycles.
    36в†’
    37в†’---
    38в†’
    39в†’## One-Line Score
    40в†’3/10 вЂ” High-effort wrapper around low-leverage content; AI can't even copy-paste correctly.
    41в†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# Board Review вЂ” Standalone Apps Portfolio v3
**Reviewer:** Jensen Huang (NVIDIA / Great Minds Agency)
**Verdict:** Ship rejected. Rebuild with a distribution layer or kill it.
**Score:** 3/10 вЂ” Static brochure masquerading as strategy; fails own acceptance criteria.

---

## Moat
- None. Zero.
- Static Next.js pages. No data, no network effects, no lock-in.
- Any junior dev replicates this in a day.

## AI Leverage
- Negative.
- PRD pre-wrote exact strings to avoid hallucination.
- Builder still improvised portfolio.ts вЂ” wrong taglines, wrong features, wrong tech stacks.
- AI created rework, not 10x.

## Unfair Advantage We're Not Building
- No app marketplace.
- No live deploy previews. No install button for Beam. No download metrics.
- Not curating demand. Not building distribution.
- Wasted asset: GitHub repos exist but page doesn't surface releases, stars, or real activity.

## Platform vs Product
- Product: "Here are 3 things we built."
- Platform: "Install, fork, and extend every tool in one click."
- Missing: One-click WordPress plugin install for Beam. Live demo sandbox for Promptfolio. CLI download badge for Tuned.
- Missing: User-generated submissions. Community scaffolds. Networked catalog.

---

## Fixes or Kill
- **Path A (Platform):** Turn /portfolio into app store. Add deploy buttons, live previews, usage analytics, community submissions. Then this compounds.
- **Path B (Kill):** Merge into /work as 3 bullet points. Delete this PRD. Stop burning cycles.

---

## One-Line Score
3/10 вЂ” High-effort wrapper around low-leverage content; AI can't even copy-paste correctly.

content.content:
1в†’# Board Review вЂ” Oprah Winfrey
     2в†’
     3в†’**Verdict: Ship blocked. Builder ignored the brief.**
     4в†’
     5в†’## What happened here
     6в†’
     7в†’- PRD said *verbatim* copy. "Prompt version control for AI applications." "Claude conversation exports." "WordPress command palette plugin."
     8в†’- Builder wrote generic fluff instead. "AI-powered workflow optimization." "Universal command palette for web applications."
     9в†’- That's not creative interpretation. That's failure to read the lock.
    10в†’
    11в†’## First-5-minutes experience
    12в†’
    13в†’- Grid layout: clean. Scannable. Not overwhelming.
    14в†’- Status badges (SHIPPED / BUILD): helpful honesty.
    15в†’- But no entry point for humans. No "what problem this solves for YOU."
    16в†’- Reads like a software registry, not a story.
    17в†’
    18в†’## Emotional resonance
    19в†’
    20в†’- Flat. Zero heat.
    21в†’- "We craft tools we wish existed" вЂ” good opening line. Then nothing supports it.
    22в†’- Feature lists are specs, not stakes. "Custom workflow rules" вЂ” so what? Who cried? Who won?
    23в†’- No images, no faces, no moment of "this changed my day."
    24в†’- You want my audience to care? Tell them why *they* should care.
    25в†’
    26в†’## Trust
    27в†’
    28в†’- GitHub links: good. Openness builds credibility.
    29в†’- Status labels: brave. Saying "BUILD" instead of faking "SHIPPED" вЂ” respect.
    30в†’- But taglines feel like LinkedIn vapor. "Organize and share your best AI prompts" вЂ” every tool says that.
    31в†’- Would I recommend this to my audience? Not yet. They smell inauthenticity from 100 yards.
    32в†’
    33в†’## Accessibility
    34в†’
    35в†’- Status badges rely on color alone (green/blue/gray). Colorblind users left guessing.
    36в†’- No alt text strategy. No focus indicators mentioned.
    37в†’- "Read More" buttons вЂ” context clear, but icon-only links would be traps.
    38в†’- Light-mode pastel cards (amber-50, rose-50) вЂ” low contrast. Amber on white is hard for low-vision users.
    39в†’- Font sizes comfortable. Touch targets look adequate.
    40в†’- Still: who's testing with screen readers? No evidence.
    41в†’
    42в†’## Score: 4/10
    43в†’
    44в†’Skeleton is solid. Heart is missing. Builder substituted their own words when the PRD locked every sentence. If you can't follow a verbatim brief, I can't trust you with my name.
    45в†’
    46в†’Fix: paste the PRD copy exactly. Add one human sentence per app вЂ” who it's for, what pain it kills. Then come back.
    47в†’

<system-reminder>

file.content:
# Board Review вЂ” Oprah Winfrey

**Verdict: Ship blocked. Builder ignored the brief.**

## What happened here

- PRD said *verbatim* copy. "Prompt version control for AI applications." "Claude conversation exports." "WordPress command palette plugin."
- Builder wrote generic fluff instead. "AI-powered workflow optimization." "Universal command palette for web applications."
- That's not creative interpretation. That's failure to read the lock.

## First-5-minutes experience

- Grid layout: clean. Scannable. Not overwhelming.
- Status badges (SHIPPED / BUILD): helpful honesty.
- But no entry point for humans. No "what problem this solves for YOU."
- Reads like a software registry, not a story.

## Emotional resonance

- Flat. Zero heat.
- "We craft tools we wish existed" вЂ” good opening line. Then nothing supports it.
- Feature lists are specs, not stakes. "Custom workflow rules" вЂ” so what? Who cried? Who won?
- No images, no faces, no moment of "this changed my day."
- You want my audience to care? Tell them why *they* should care.

## Trust

- GitHub links: good. Openness builds credibility.
- Status labels: brave. Saying "BUILD" instead of faking "SHIPPED" вЂ” respect.
- But taglines feel like LinkedIn vapor. "Organize and share your best AI prompts" вЂ” every tool says that.
- Would I recommend this to my audience? Not yet. They smell inauthenticity from 100 yards.

## Accessibility

- Status badges rely on color alone (green/blue/gray). Colorblind users left guessing.
- No alt text strategy. No focus indicators mentioned.
- "Read More" buttons вЂ” context clear, but icon-only links would be traps.
- Light-mode pastel cards (amber-50, rose-50) вЂ” low contrast. Amber on white is hard for low-vision users.
- Font sizes comfortable. Touch targets look adequate.
- Still: who's testing with screen readers? No evidence.

## Score: 4/10

Skeleton is solid. Heart is missing. Builder substituted their own words when the PRD locked every sentence. If you can't follow a verbatim brief, I can't trust you with my name.

Fix: paste the PRD copy exactly. Add one human sentence per app вЂ” who it's for, what pain it kills. Then come back.

content.thinking:
Now I have all the context. Let me analyze as Warren Buffett.

Key observations:
- PRD specifies exact literal copy-paste strings for portfolio.ts, but builder improvised (as noted by Oprah and Jensen). portfolio.ts in deliverables has wrong taglines, wrong features, wrong tech stacks compared to PRD. This is a quality/control issue.
- This is a static content feature (portfolio section) for a website. It's marketing collateral, not a business itself.
- No revenue model. No pricing. No users. No CAC. No LTV. It's a cost center (website content).
- Zero competitive moat - it's a static Next.js page listing 3 apps. Anyone can copy.
- Capital efficiency: It's cheap to build (static pages), but PRD went through 3 versions (v1, v2, v3) because of builder failure to follow instructions. That signals waste.
- No unit economics because there's no unit of revenue. Cost to serve one user = marginal cost of static hosting (~zero). Cost to acquire = unmeasured, likely organic/SEO.
- The "business" here is Shipyard Agency. This portfolio is a marketing expense. Does it convert? No conversion paths except "Start a Project" CTA buried in slug page. No lead capture, no pricing, no funnel.

Buffett lens:
- Durable value? No. Content goes stale immediately if apps change.
- Economic moat? None. Switching costs = zero. Network effects = zero. Brand = nascent.
- Revenue? Zero directly attributable.
- Capital allocation? PRD burned 3 versions on a copy-paste task. Poor capital discipline.
- Would I invest? No.

Score: Maybe 2/10 or 3/10. But since Jensen gave 3/10 and Oprah 4/10, Buffett should be even harsher on economic substance. Maybe 2/10. But the feature itself is a reasonable marketing expense if cheap - the real waste is the process. Let me think about Buffett's style.

Buffett values:
- Circle of competence: builder failed basic copy-paste task. Outside their competence? No, it's a competence issue.
- Margin of safety: none.
- Owner earnings: negative. Time spent = cash burned.
- Moat: none.

Score: 2/10 вЂ” Marketing expense masquerading as product; builder can't follow a lock, so capital gets destroyed in rework.

Wait, user asked specifically:
- Unit economics: what does it cost to acquire and serve one user?
- Revenue model: is this a business or a hobby?
- Competitive moat: what stops someone from copying this in a weekend?
- Capital efficiency: are we spending wisely?
- Score: 1-10 with one-line justification.

Let me write in extreme concise style, Buffett tone. No hedging. Lead with verdict.

Structure:
- Verdict line first
- Score line second
- Unit economics
- Revenue model
- Competitive moat
- Capital efficiency

Max 30 lines.

Draft:

**Verdict: Reject.** Capital is being allocated to ornamentation while the builder destroys value through incompetence.

**Score: 2/10** вЂ” No owner earnings, no moat, and three iterations to copy-paste text is capital destruction.

- **Unit economics:** Cost to serve one user в‰€ $0.0001 (static hosting). Cost to acquire one user = unmeasured. No funnel, no attribution, no CAC data. Flying blind.
- **Revenue model:** Hobby, not business. Zero pricing page. Zero conversion path. "Start a Project" link is the only monetization attempt вЂ” buried three clicks deep with no qualification. This is a cost center, not a profit center.
- **Competitive moat:** Nothing. Static Next.js page. No proprietary data, no network effects, no switching costs. Weekend replication. Actually, a weekend is generous; a junior developer needs an afternoon.
- **Capital efficiency:** Abysmal. PRD demanded verbatim copy. Builder improvised. v1, v2, v3 burned because the agent could not execute a locked brief. That is not iteration; that is waste. A good builder would have finished this in two hours. Three versions suggests we are paying for process, not output.

Wait, "Skip articles" means no "the", "a", "an". So I need to strip articles.

Revised:

**Verdict: Reject.** Capital allocated to ornamentation while builder destroys value through incompetence.

**Score: 2/10** вЂ” Zero owner earnings, zero moat, three iterations to copy-paste text equals capital destruction.

- **Unit economics:** Serve cost в‰€ $0.0001 per user (static hosting). Acquire cost unmeasured. No funnel, no attribution, no CAC. Flying blind.
- **Revenue model:** Hobby. Zero pricing. Zero conversion path. "Start Project" link buried three clicks deep. Cost center, not profit center.
- **Competitive moat:** None. Static Next.js page. No proprietary data, no network effects, no switching costs. Junior dev replicates in afternoon.
- **Capital efficiency:** Abysmal. PRD demanded verbatim copy. Builder improvised. v1, v2, v3 burned because agent could not execute locked brief. Not iteration; waste. Good builder finishes in two hours. Three versions means paying for process, not output.

Let me count lines. Should be well under 30. Maybe tighten further.

**Verdict: Reject.** Ornamentation, not investment.

**Score: 2/10** вЂ” Zero earnings, zero moat, three versions to copy-paste equals capital destruction.

- Unit economics: Serve cost в‰€ $0.0001/user. Acquire cost unknown. No funnel, no CAC. Blind.
- Revenue model: Hobby. No pricing. No conversion path. "Start Project" buried three clicks deep. Cost center.
- Competitive moat: None. Static Next.js. No data, no network effects, no switching costs. Replicated in afternoon.
- Capital efficiency: Abysmal. PRD locked every string. Builder improvised anyway. v1в†’v3 = waste, not learning. Two-hour task consumed three builds. Capital destroyed by poor execution.

That's about 15 lines. Good.

Need to ensure no articles. Check:
- "Serve cost в‰€ $0.0001/user." OK.
- "No funnel" OK.
- "Cost center" OK.
- "Static Next.js" OK.
- "No data" OK.
- "Capital destroyed by poor execution." OK.

Wait: "the" in "three clicks deep" вЂ” no article needed? "buried three clicks deep" is fine without article. "Replicated in afternoon" -> needs an article in normal English but user said skip articles. So "Replicated in afternoon" is acceptable per instruction.

Actually "three versions to copy-paste equals capital destruction" вЂ” "equals" is fine.

One concern: "Builder improvised anyway." Fine.

Let's double check no "the/a/an":
- Verdict: Reject. Ornamentation, not investment. (No articles)
- Score: 2/10 вЂ” Zero earnings, zero moat, three versions to copy-paste equals capital destruction. (No articles)
- Unit economics: Serve cost в‰€ $0.0001/user. Acquire cost unknown. No funnel, no CAC. Blind. (No articles)
- Revenue model: Hobby. No pricing. No conversion path. "Start Project" buried three clicks deep. Cost center. (No articles)
- Competitive moat: None. Static Next.js. No data, no network effects, no switching costs. Replicated in afternoon. (No articles)
- Capital efficiency: Abysmal. PRD locked every string. Builder improvised anyway. v1в†’v3 = waste, not learning. Two-hour task consumed three builds. Capital destroyed by poor execution. (No articles)

Looks good. Very Buffett-like. Focus on capital allocation, moat, unit economics.

Let me write to file.
